shots.action: A crew member hauls the extension ladder off the truck rack in the yard.
Inside, he sets the feet on the wet floor by the loading door - no mat, no footing check.
shots.vo_line: The floor had been mopped four minutes earlier.

shots.action: The ladder leans too steep, nowhere near a four to one angle, rails flexing as he starts to climb.
shots.vo_line: Four to one. The rule fits in four words.

shots.action: Three rungs from the top he leans into an overreach, belt buckle past the rail, fingers stretching for the junction box.
shots.vo_line: Six more inches. Or thirty seconds to move the ladder.

shots.action: The feet lose grip - the ladder slide begins - he rides it down as it skates off the wet concrete.
Corrective replay: feet on dry footing, a proper angle, three points of contact all the way, tie off at the top.
shots.vo_line: Same job. Sixteen minutes. Done once.
